name: Comedy
description: Comedy films, sitcoms, and the stars who made us laugh

questions.q: In the 1993 film 'Groundhog Day', which actor plays a weatherman stuck reliving the same day?

source.notes: Directed by Harold Ramis; set in Punxsutawney, Pennsylvania.

questions.q: Which Robin Williams comedy has him disguising himself as an elderly Scottish nanny to spend time with his children?

source.notes: 1993 film directed by Chris Columbus.

questions.q: Eddie Murphy plays Detective Axel Foley, a Detroit cop in Los Angeles, in which franchise?

source.notes: Original film released 1984; series spans to 2024.

questions.q: In 'Back to the Future', Doc Brown converts what kind of car into a time machine?

source.notes: Stainless-steel DeLorean with gull-wing doors.

questions.q: Ghostbusters (1984) is set in which major U.S. city overrun by paranormal activity?

source.notes: Features the Stay Puft Marshmallow Man rampaging through Manhattan.

questions.q: Billy Crystal and Meg Ryan played the leads in which 1989 romantic comedy?

source.notes: Directed by Rob Reiner; iconic 'I'll have what she's having' scene.

questions.q: The disaster-parody film 'Airplane!' is a spoof of what genre?

source.notes: 1980 film; spoofs 'Zero Hour!' (1957) and 1970s disaster movies.

questions.q: In 'Back to the Future Part II', Marty and Doc travel to which future year?

source.notes: Specifically October 21, 2015, celebrated as 'Back to the Future Day'.

questions.q: In 'Monty Python and the Holy Grail', the knights famously substitute what for horses?

source.notes: Banged together to simulate hoof-beats — a low-budget solution that became iconic.

questions.q: Steve Carell starred as the title character in which 2005 Judd Apatow comedy?

source.notes: Apatow's directorial debut; Carell co-wrote the screenplay.

questions.q: Matthew Broderick plays the title truant in which 1986 John Hughes film about skipping school?

source.notes: Features the famous 'Twist and Shout' parade scene in Chicago.

questions.q: Michael Keaton plays the mischievous title ghost in which 1988 Tim Burton comedy?

source.notes: 'It's showtime!'

questions.q: Which filmmaker directed the irreverent 1974 western comedy 'Blazing Saddles'?

source.notes: Brooks also directed 'Young Frankenstein' the same year.

questions.q: Will Ferrell plays the evil fashion mogul Jacobim Mugatu in which 2001 parody comedy?

source.notes: Starring and directed by Ben Stiller.

questions.q: Sacha Baron Cohen created which comedy character — a faux British wannabe rapper — that got a feature film in 2002?

source.notes: Debuted on 'The 11 O'Clock Show' in 1998; got his own movie 'Ali G Indahouse' in 2002.

questions.q: Which Hollywood star plays Hugh Grant's love interest in the 1999 romantic comedy 'Notting Hill'?

source.notes: Roberts plays a famous American actress who falls for a London bookshop owner.

questions.q: In 1979's 'The Jerk', which comedian plays naive protagonist Navin R. Johnson?

source.notes: Directed by Carl Reiner; Martin's first starring role.

questions.q: The country club setting in the 1980 golf comedy 'Caddyshack' is called what?

source.notes: Directed by Harold Ramis; stars Chevy Chase and Bill Murray.

questions.q: Charlie Chaplin's 1925 silent comedy classic, famous for the Thanksgiving boiled-shoe scene, is titled what?

source.notes: Set during the Klondike Gold Rush in the Yukon.

questions.q: In the 1994 movie 'Dumb and Dumber', what Colorado destination are Lloyd and Harry trying to reach?

source.notes: Jim Carrey and Jeff Daniels star as the titular duo.

questions.q: Which aging Western star won a Best Supporting Actor Oscar at age 73 for 'City Slickers' (1991)?

source.notes: Famously did one-arm push-ups on stage during his acceptance speech.

questions.q: Rick Moranis played Dark-Helmet in the 1987 Mel Brooks parody of 'Star Wars'. What is the name of that film?

source.notes: Also stars Bill Pullman as Lone Starr and Mel Brooks as President Skroob.
